name: Denzil Best
abstract: Denzil DaCosta Best (April 27, 1917 – May 24, 1965) was an American jazz percussionist and composer born in New York City. He was a prominent bebop drummer in the 1950s and early 1960s.

concerts.label: Horizons in Jazz featuring Dizzy Gillespie, George Shearing, and Others

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: if you want Jimmy Jones and John Levy and Denzil Best, you’ve got a rhythm section here.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: I had Dexter Gordon, Curly Russell and Denzil Best on drums, Jimmy Jones was on piano.

about_quotes.speaker: Sheila Jordan
about_quotes.evidence: he said, “No, no. We‟re gonna use guitar. Barry Galbraith, Denzil Best and Steve Swallow on acoustic bass.” ... Denzil was… You know what Denzil said? [laughs] What he said to George? He said, “She scares me.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheila Jordan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I was playing piano, with Lawrence Brown, Johnny Hodges, Al Hall the bass player, Denzil Best the drummer, and Billy Strayhorn was playing congas.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: December 15th, 1944, Mary Lou recorded three numbers for Asch with seven musicians which included ... Denzil Best on drums and me.

about_quotes.speaker: DeFranco Buddy
about_quotes.evidence: George Shearing and I, and John Levy, Denzil Best; we had a quartet at a club called the Clique.
about_quotes.doc_title: DeFranco Buddy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
